criteria: cloud-API or PCIe direct access feasibility

ranking.rationale: PCIe standard card form factor + ONNX/PyTorch/TVM/OpenCL stack (most standardized SDK among the 4 vendors). 44 paying customers in early 2026, HK IPO 2026-04 600x oversubscribed. ONNX hidden-state hook feasibility is highest (TVM compile-pass annotations controllable). Single PCIe card eval is the most plausible $0-low-budget path even though pricing undisclosed.

TFLNoI native analog with C/C++/Python PCIe API -- highest direct programmability rating. 2026-Q1 shipments, Leibniz LRZ first installation (world's first photonic AI in supercomputing facility) -- institutional academic partnership path possible. On-chip enhanced nonlinear units mean Phi measurement on Q.ANT carries genuine substrate signal (not just linear projection). Drawback: shortest commercial track record, undisclosed pricing.

Largest funding ($4.4B valuation, $850M total raised), Nature 2025-04 light-speed matmul paper -- highest academic credibility. Idiom compiler bridges PyTorch. Decisive blocker: 4U blade form factor only, no single dev kit, early-access partnership mandatory. For anima setup the cloud API absence is decisive blocker -- vision is 1st but actionability is 3rd.

excluded.reason: Datacenter switch / hyperscaler-only access model, no anima user direct path
excluded.alternative_route: NTT Research PHI Lab (programmable photonics, SPIE 2024 demo) academic partnership only

second_contact_target: Q.ANT (LRZ academic collaboration via Leibniz Supercomputing Centre install)
third_contact_target: Lightmatter (Envise early-access partnership; longer timeline, larger commitment)
